reasoning: Phase B Task B4: per-request `SET LOCAL search_path` in all wizard API routes. Adds a `withTenantSearchPath(pool, tenantSchema, fn)` helper in `apps/wizard/lib/tenants/search-path.ts` that wraps BEGIN / SET LOCAL search_path / fn / COMMIT with ROLLBACK-on-throw and release-in-finally. All four wizard route files (`[projectId]/state`, `proposal/generate`, `interview/answer`, `interview/complete`) now flow per-tenant pg work through the helper; in-memory V1 stub paths intentionally skip the wrap to avoid pool-client acquisition. Reuses the existing `@caia/atlas-design-snapshotter` SET LOCAL pattern verbatim (same `quoteIdent` regex, same transaction shape). Extends `apps/wizard/lib/tenants/*` and `apps/wizard/lib/wizard/store-wire.ts` in place; no new pg/db layer introduced. 19 vitest cases (target ≥10) cover happy path, rollback-on-throw, release-on-throw, release-on-rollback-throw, concurrent-no-bleed, transaction-scoping-after-commit, empty-schema rejection, SQL-injection guard (quotes/hyphen/space/leading-digit), pool-exhaustion, return-value propagation, and the explicit-external-transaction passthrough. Full wizard suite: 231 passed, 0 failed.

reuseSearchResults.considered_for: BEGIN / SET LOCAL search_path / fn / COMMIT wrapper
reuseSearchResults.reason: packages/atlas-design-snapshotter/src/snapshotter.ts:145 already issues `SET LOCAL search_path = ${quoteIdent(schema)}, public` inside a connection-scoped transaction; the helper mirrors the same regex (`^[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*$`) and rollback-on-throw shape so the two places agree on what a safe tenant schema looks like.

reuseSearchResults.considered_for: module home for the new helper
reuseSearchResults.reason: Brief mandates extending `apps/wizard/lib/tenants/` patterns rather than introducing a new db layer. The helper lives next to `store.ts`/`wire.ts`/`provision.ts`/`infisical.ts` so the wizard's tenant-isolation surface stays in one place.

store-wire already loads `tenants.schema_name` once per tenant and caches the StateStore. We extracted the lookup so the same one-shot query feeds both `getStateStoreForTenant` and the new `resolveTenantSchema`. No double round-trip.
Same driver everywhere in `apps/wizard`. The helper accepts a minimal `WithTenantSearchPathPool` interface so tests don't need the full `pg.Pool` type surface.
Listed in AGENTS.md but no such package ships on develop. PLAN.md §5 tracks the swap when the package lands.

Not in the workspace; the mocked `Pool`/`PoolClient` interface is enough to assert BEGIN/SET LOCAL/COMMIT order, rollback-on-throw, release-in-finally, concurrent isolation, and the external-transaction passthrough.
Pure-server task; no UI surface touched.
The interview routes continue to wrap work in `tracer.withSpan(...)`. We add `wizard.tenant_schema` as a span attribute on the live path so traces show which tenant got pinned.
Bus wiring unchanged; lifecycle events for FSM transitions are B5.

`PgStateStore.transitionAtomic` keeps its own BEGIN/COMMIT for the FSM tables in the `caia_meta` schema. Those queries are schema-qualified and don't need the tenant search_path; our wrapper covers the surrounding read/write.

requested_modifications: Wave 2: when the live interview path is wired, drop the conditional `tenantSchema | null` branch in `interview/answer/route.ts` — `withTenantSearchPath` should run on every request unconditionally.
Wave 2: when `@chiefaia/persistence-postgres` lands, replace the `pg` import in `apps/wizard/lib/tenants/search-path.ts` with the canonical wrapper.
_note: Stub critic verdict per the Phase B operator policy (CAIA_EA_STUB=1 path) — live EA review can be replayed via scripts/submit-plan.mjs against the merged commit. Status downgraded to 'approved-with-modifications' to surface the two Wave-2 follow-ups.
